# Test fixtures

Binary test fixtures that don't fit naturally into the JSONL or
hand-crafted bytes of the test files themselves.

## `sample-soc2.pdf`

A tiny synthetic SOC 2 Type II attestation report (2 pages, ~2.3 KB).
Used by `tests/test_ingestion.py` to exercise `PDFReader` against a
realistic PDF input.

The vendor name ("Acme Analytics Inc.") and content are entirely
synthetic; no real SOC 2 report content appears in this file.

### Regenerating the fixture

If this file is lost or needs updating, regenerate it with:

```python
import io
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas

buf = io.BytesIO()
c = canvas.Canvas(buf, pagesize=letter)

# Page 1: cover and overview
c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 14)
c.drawString(72, 720, "Sample SOC 2 Type II Attestation Report")
c.setFont("Helvetica", 11)
c.drawString(72, 690, "Vendor: Acme Analytics Inc.")
c.drawString(72, 670, "Reporting Period: 2026-01-01 to 2026-12-31")
c.drawString(72, 640, "Trust Service Categories:")
c.drawString(96, 620, "- Security")
c.drawString(96, 600, "- Availability")
c.drawString(96, 580, "- Confidentiality")
c.drawString(72, 540, "Auditor Opinion: Unqualified")
c.showPage()

# Page 2: control activities
c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 12)
c.drawString(72, 720, "Section 2: Control Activities")
c.setFont("Helvetica", 10)
c.drawString(72, 690, "CC6.1 Logical Access: Multi-factor authentication is enforced.")
c.drawString(72, 670, "CC7.2 System Monitoring: Continuous detection in place.")
c.drawString(72, 650, "CC8.1 Change Management: All changes require peer review.")
c.showPage()

c.save()
with open("tests/fixtures/sample-soc2.pdf", "wb") as f:
    f.write(buf.getvalue())
```

`reportlab` is not a runtime or dev dependency of this project; install
it ad-hoc only when regenerating fixtures: `pip install reportlab`.

If the regenerated bytes differ from the committed file (different
reportlab version, different platform), tests asserting specific
content_hash values will fail. Update those assertions to use the new
hash; the fixture is the source of truth, not the hash.

## `sample-regulation.pdf`

A tiny synthetic regulation document (3 pages, ~3.3 KB). Used by
`tests/test_retrieval.py` to exercise `CorpusLoader` and the
end-to-end retrieval pipeline against a realistic regulation-style PDF.

The content is entirely synthetic (a made-up "Model Risk Management
Guideline" with sections on scope, requirements, and vendor concerns)
and does not reproduce any real regulation text. The synthetic content
mirrors the structure of real regulator guidance (numbered sections,
requirements, vendor model risk language) so retrieval tests
demonstrate realistic relevance ranking.

### Regenerating

```python
import io
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas

buf = io.BytesIO()
c = canvas.Canvas(buf, pagesize=letter)

# Page 1: scope and definitions
c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 14)
c.drawString(72, 720, "Sample Regulation: Model Risk Management Guideline")
c.setFont("Helvetica", 10)
c.drawString(72, 690, "Section 1: Scope")
c.drawString(72, 670, "This synthetic guideline applies to AI systems used for regulated decisions")
c.drawString(72, 655, "within federally regulated financial institutions.")
c.drawString(72, 625, "Section 2: Definitions")
c.drawString(72, 605, "Regulated decisions means decisions affecting consumers in credit,")
c.drawString(72, 590, "insurance underwriting, or other supervised activities.")
c.showPage()

# Page 2: requirements
c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 12)
c.drawString(72, 720, "Section 3: Requirements")
c.setFont("Helvetica", 10)
c.drawString(72, 690, "3.1 Model inventory: institutions shall maintain an inventory of all")
c.drawString(72, 675, "AI models in scope of this guideline including vendor-supplied models.")
c.drawString(72, 645, "3.2 Risk assessment: each model shall be assessed against impact tier and")
c.drawString(72, 630, "subject to proportionate validation before deployment.")
c.drawString(72, 600, "3.3 Ongoing monitoring: deployed models shall be monitored for")
c.drawString(72, 585, "performance drift and reviewed at least annually.")
c.showPage()

# Page 3: vendor model risk
c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 12)
c.drawString(72, 720, "Section 4: Vendor Model Risk")
c.setFont("Helvetica", 10)
c.drawString(72, 690, "4.1 Third party AI systems are subject to the same risk requirements")
c.drawString(72, 675, "as internally developed systems regardless of vendor representations.")
c.drawString(72, 645, "4.2 Institutions shall obtain SOC 2 attestation or equivalent evidence of")
c.drawString(72, 630, "operational controls from material AI vendors before integration.")
c.showPage()

c.save()
with open("tests/fixtures/sample-regulation.pdf", "wb") as f:
    f.write(buf.getvalue())
```
